diagrams.title: Block Matrix Partitioning
diagrams.description: Shows a 2×2 block matrix with blocks A, B, C, D in quadrants
elements: Diagonal elements A, D determine eigenvalues in triangular case
Off-diagonal B, C don't affect eigenvalues in block triangular
diagrams.purpose: Understanding how block structure affects eigenvalues - eigenvalues come only from diagonal blocks in block triangular matrices

diagrams.title: Characteristic Polynomial Visualization
diagrams.description: Shows the relationship between matrix A, subtracting λ from diagonal, and computing determinant
diagrams.purpose: Visualizing how characteristic polynomial is constructed step by step from the matrix

diagrams.title: Eigenvalue Spectrum of Block Diagonal
diagrams.description: Shows two separate blocks with their eigenvalues combining into full spectrum
elements: Block 1 with eigenvalues λ₁, λ₂, λ₃
Combined spectrum: {λ₁, λ₂, λ₃, μ₁, μ₂}
diagrams.purpose: Understanding that eigenvalues of block diagonal matrices are union of eigenvalues of each diagonal block

diagrams.title: Schur Complement Geometry
diagrams.description: Shows block matrix with Schur complement computation visualized as 'elimination' of first block
elements: Original block matrix [A B; C D]
Resulting Schur complement S = D - CA⁻¹B
diagrams.purpose: Understanding Sylvester's determinant theorem visually through block elimination
